title: poa pfisteri
intro: Poa pfisteri, also known as Pfister's meadow-grass, is a species of grass native to New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Poa pfisteri include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory health, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and antimicrobial agent. Poa pfisteri can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional Maori medicine to treat various ailments.
